t:
@amjadt25 "Somaliland… taariikhdu ma garaacayso albaabka oo keliya, waxay u soo socotaa sidii hir aan la joojin karin."

Aamiin!
🫡
exp: The post is in Somali and translates to 'Somaliland… history is not just knocking on the door, it is coming like an unstoppable wave.' It expresses strong support for Somaliland's independence or recognition, using metaphorical language. This is a political statement, not hate speech.

t:
At #MaskaniKisumu, young advocates are identifying real digital threats affecting their organisations, including phishing attacks, account breaches, misinformation, and targeted harassment that can weaken online advocacy efforts. Strengthening awareness is key to building safer civic spaces.
#Maskan
exp: This post discusses digital threats and online advocacy efforts in Kisumu, Kenya, identifying issues like phishing and misinformation. It is an informative post about cybersecurity and advocacy, not hate speech.

t: @M_Sungwa Trust no one! Hizi mambo za unatua nchini Tz unatangaza, au unaingia mkoa fulani unapost “aliyeko Dodoma tutafutane”, unaingia mikononi mwa wauaji in a matter of seconds! Trust no one!
exp: The post, in Swahili, translates to a warning about personal safety and the dangers of sharing location information online, advising against trusting strangers. It is general safety advice and not hate speech.

t: @OJ_Smoke_ 1/You’re shifting the goalposts. Your original quote-tweet was about Somali and Kyrgyz soldiers training, yet you called them kuffar. Don’t hide behind foreign rulers now after making blanket takfir on two muslim soldiers in Muslim country.
exp: This post criticizes another user for calling Somali and Kyrgyz soldiers 'kuffar' (infidels/disbelievers) and performing 'takfir' (declaring them apostates). It is counter-speech, condemning the use of religious slurs rather than using them.

t: Today, we honor the tears, sacrifices, and courage of our parents, the men and women who took up arms against oppression in Sudan for a better future for South Sudan. It is not yet what they dreamed of, but it will be. 🇸🇸 Happy 16th May #SouthSudan
exp: This post honors the sacrifices of those who fought for South Sudan's liberation. It is a commemorative and nationalistic message, not hate speech.

t:

Even though you are no longer being paid proportionally by the independent nation y
exp: This post celebrates SPLA Day, acknowledging the struggle and lives lost for South Sudan's independence. It is a historical and commemorative post, not hate speech.

t:
Diaspora cash inflows drop Sh6.8 billion as inflation squeezes Kenyans abroad

https://t.co/yfRYOhWQL5
exp: This post shares a news headline about a drop in diaspora cash inflows to Kenya. It is a factual news report and not hate speech.

t:
Remittances to Kenya eased to KSh 51.39Bn (US$ 397.78Mn) in April 2026, falling 5.9% YoY from US$ 422.89Mn in April 2025.

➠ North America: US$ 207.6Mn (KSh 26.8Bn) 
➠ Europe: US$ 81.8Mn (KSh 10.6Bn)
➠ Rest of World: US$ 108.4Mn (KSh 14.0Bn)

Jan-Apr 2026 sits at KSh 216.02Bn (US$ 1.67Bn), up 1.0% Y
exp: This post is a factual report detailing economic data regarding remittances to Kenya. It contains no hateful language, slurs, or attacks against any specific group or individual.
